_meta.note: Human-authored clarifying notes merged on top of the official NCRB mappings by scripts/ingest_ncrb_mappings.py. This file is NEVER overwritten by the ingest, so re-running is idempotent. It carries nuance the bare correspondence table can't (e.g. that BNS 152 is a NEW offence, not a sedition successor). law_map.py ignores this file (no from_code/to_code).

124A.note: Sedition (IPC 124A) was repealed without a direct successor. BNS s.152 (acts endangering sovereignty, unity and integrity of India) is a NEW, differently-framed offence often discussed in its place — not a one-to-one re-enactment.

309.note: Attempt to suicide is not retained as a general offence (see the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017). BNS s.226 covers attempt to suicide to compel or restrain a public servant.

377.note: No direct equivalent retained in the BNS.

498A.note: BNS s.86 defines 'cruelty' for the purposes of this offence.

506.note: Aggravated forms of criminal intimidation fall under BNS s.351(3).

156.note: A Magistrate's power to order investigation (CrPC 156(3)) corresponds to BNSS s.175(3).

25.note: The related discovery rule (IEA s.27) folds into BSA s.23(2).
